observations.title: Judgment Day approved: consolidation to gentle-ai SDD
observations.content:
**What**: Ran 3-round Judgment Day on consolidation commit 18cb3e4b. Round 1 found 4 confirmed issues (1 CRITICAL: exposed API key in .bak file, 3 WARNING: missing deprecation notices). Round 2 found 1 more missed file. Round 3: both judges returned CLEAN.

**Why**: Needed adversarial verification before merging the consolidation that removed old agent systems.

**Where**: .opencode/opencode.jsonc.bak (deleted), docs/superclaude-codigos/ (3 files got DEPRECATED notices), docs/superpowers/ (4 files got SUPERSEDED notices)

**Learned**: 
- When adding deprecation notices, grep the ENTIRE directory not just files you know about — missed superclaude-comandos-desarrollo.md in first pass
- Judgment Day is effective: Round 1 found a CRITICAL security issue (API key in plaintext) that would have b

observations.title: Completed portfolio redesign with WOW factor
observations.content:
**What**: Rewrote all page templates (index.astro, [slug].astro, news/index.astro) to use the design system CSS variables instead of hardcoded zinc-* Tailwind classes. Replaced all emoji icons with inline SVGs. Added WOW-factor hero with gradient mesh background, grid pattern overlay, status badge, and scroll indicator.

**Why**: User said the site "se ve demasiado básica" — needed to level up the visual design to match modern dark mode UI patterns (Linear/Vercel style).

**Where**: portfolio/src/pages/index.astro, portfolio/src/pages/projects/[slug].astro, portfolio/src/pages/news/index.astro, portfolio/public/favicon.svg

**Learned**: 
- Using inline `style` with CSS vars + `onmouseover`/`onmouseout` for hover states avoids Tailwind class proliferation with CSS variables
- Glass-card cla

observations.title: sdd-init/examya
observations.content:
## Project Context: examya

**Stack**: Next.js 14 (web), NestJS 11 (api + agents), Prisma 6 + PostgreSQL + pgvector, Flutter (mobile), pnpm workspaces
**Architecture**: DDD, Clean Architecture, monorepo `apps/*` + `packages/*`
**Testing**: Vitest (unit), Supertest+real-DB (integration), Playwright (E2E), DeepEval/Python (LLM evaluation)
**Strict TDD Mode**: enabled (project has full test runner + TDD rule in CLAUDE.md for tasks ID≥8)
**CI/CD**: GitHub Actions, Railway
**Languages**: TypeScript (NestJS/Next.js), Python (evaluation layer), Dart (Flutter)
**Key conventions**: Conventional commits `feat(scope): Task: N.M desc`, comments in English, user comms in Spanish, pnpm -F for workspace filters
**Monorepo structure**:
- apps/api — NestJS backend port 3001
- apps/agents — NestJS agents mi

observations.title: Implemented Astro native i18n ES/EN with URL routing
observations.content:
**What**: Added full ES/EN internationalization to the Astro 6 portfolio using URL-based routing (no JS toggle). Spanish at `/`, English at `/en/`.

**Why**: SEO-friendly i18n — Google indexes both languages independently. Client-side toggle was rejected.

**Where**:
- `src/i18n/ui.ts` — all translation strings (ES + EN)
- `src/i18n/utils.ts` — `getLang(pathname)`, `useTranslations(lang)`, `getAlternatePath()`, `basePath()`
- `src/components/LangSwitch.astro` — ES/EN pill toggle in nav (desktop + mobile)
- `src/layouts/Base.astro` — detects lang from URL, adds `<html lang>`, hreflang SEO tags, LangSwitch
- `src/content.config.ts` — added `lang: z.enum(['es','en']).default('es')` and `canonicalSlug` to blog schema
- `src/pages/index.astro` — uses `useTranslations('es')`
- `src/pages/blog/in

observations.title: New skills: debug-prod and examya-test
observations.content:
**What**: Created two new Claude Code skills for Examya.

**Why**: Repeated production debugging sessions with manual log sharing — needed a repeatable protocol.

**Where**: 
- `~/.claude/skills/debug-prod/SKILL.md` — production WhatsApp bot debugging via Railway CLI
- `~/.claude/skills/examya-test/SKILL.md` — test creation guide for both workspaces

**Learned**:
- debug-prod skill: service routing (examya-agents for bot behavior, examya-api for orders/payments), Railway commands (`railway logs -n 400 --service examya-agents`), known failure patterns with root causes
- examya-test skill covers both workspaces: agents (Vitest, pipeline pattern, FSM injection) and api (NestJS TestingModule, supertest, Prisma mockDeep, domain tests)
- Railway CLI: `railway status` shows current linked service

**Learned**: 
- engram sync --all (no sin flag) para exportar todos los proyectos desde el vault git
- Canal de sync = obsidian-vault repo en GitHub, chunks en .engram/chunks/*.jsonl.gz sin conflictos

observations.title: Created E2E WhatsApp order flow integration tests
observations.content:
**What**: Created comprehensive integration tests that simulate complete multi-step WhatsApp conversations for the medical order flow. All 13 tests pass, no regressions.

**Why**: Previous test coverage only tested isolated fragments. No test verified the full chain: photo → OCR → pendingOcr capture → RUT/email messages → order creation → payment link → post-payment delivery.

**Where**: `apps/api/src/modules/whatsapp/controllers/__tests__/whatsapp-order-flow.spec.ts`

**Learned**:
- Stateful in-memory contextStore: `Map<string, string>` simulates `prismaAgents.whatsappContext`. savePendingOcr writes to it, getPendingOcr reads from it, clearPendingOcr deletes the key — all within a single test.
- Stateful mutable user: `Object.assign(user, data)` in `prisma.user.update` mock allows `update

**Learned**:

observations.title: Flow separation analysis: OCR Quotation vs Shuri Purchase — hardening applied
observations.content:
## What
Complete analysis and hardening of the OCR Quotation vs Shuri Purchase flow separation in the WhatsApp bot.

## Why
User asked to verify both flows don't overlap at intent detection, pricing, or PDF generation. Discovered a state contamination risk in PurchaseHandler and a pre-existing coupon discount bug.

## Flow Separation (confirmed independent)

**OCR Quotation flow** (IMAGE message):
- Entry: `handleImageMessage()` → `handleOcrExtraction()` in `whatsapp-webhook.controller.ts`
- Price: `$2,000` from `WHATSAPP_QUOTE_PRICE` env var (read in controller, NOT in agents)
- Creates order with `isQuotation: true`
- PDF: `mapSaleOrder(..., isQuotation=true)` → title "COTIZACIÓN DE EXÁMENES", FONASA pricing (Nacional 3 levels / Magallanes level 3), no doctor section
- pendingOcr pattern

observations.title: Arquitectura sync bidireccional Engram Mac ↔ VPS Cotocha
observations.content:
**What**: Sistema de sync bidireccional de memorias entre Engram local (Mac) y Cotocha (VPS) vía git repo obsidian-vault como transporte.

**Flujo Mac → VPS**: engram-sync.sh --sync-only → exporta chunk → git push obsidian-vault main → VPS cron cada 2h hace git pull + engram sync --import → bridge-engram-to-qmd.py escribe .md en workspace/memory/mac/ → QMD indexa en 5min.

**Flujo VPS → Mac**: bridge-openclaw-engram.py (Option C: parsea session JSONL de /root/.openclaw/agents/main/sessions/ + .md files de otros agents) → genera chunk formato {sessions, observations, prompts} como un solo JSON dentro del .jsonl.gz (no JSONL) → git push origin main → engram-sync.sh en Mac absorbe automáticamente a las 7:30am.

**Crons VPS**: bridge export cada 4h (xx:30 UTC), engram import cada 2h (xx:00 UTC

Nivel 2 — _raw/ poblado:
- 5 artículos descargados como .md en 04_Investigacion/Interoperabilidad-Salud-IK/_raw/: karpathy-venturebeat, ley-21668, fhir-connectathon-2026, hl7-ai-office, mcp-vs-a2a-clarifai
- El pipeline raw/ → wiki/ ahora tiene fuentes reales (antes solo re

observations.title: Git vault unificado — reset --soft resolvió historial no relacionado Mac vs Cotocha
observations.content:
What: El repo local del vault Obsidian tenía solo 4 commits sin ancestro común con el remote (Cotocha/VPS tenía 133). Git rechazaba merge y rebase fallaba commit-por-commit por conflictos en archivos compartidos (diario, _INDEX, manifest.json).

Why: El Obsidian Git plugin re-inicializó el repo local en algún momento, perdiendo la historia compartida. Los 4 commits locales eran "sync: vault" automáticos del plugin sin relación al árbol de Cotocha.

Solution: git reset --soft obsidian-vault/main → todos los cambios locales quedaron staged sobre el HEAD del remote → un commit limpio encima → push exitoso. Resultado: rama sincronizada, 0 commits de diferencia.

Learned: Para repos de sync divergidos sin ancestro común, NO usar rebase (causa N rondas de conflictos). Usar reset --soft + commit

observations.title: Production deploy: pg_trgm + MINSAL guides + interpreter fixes
observations.content:
**What**: Deployed interpreter infrastructure to Supabase production (examya_agents):
1. Installed pg_trgm extension (was missing — similarity() fallback would fail)
2. Seeded 5 MINSAL GPC guides into medical_guides table (hemogram, lipid, glucose, thyroid, urine)
3. Created formal migration file for pg_trgm (20260309000001_add_pg_trgm_extension)
4. Updated schema.prisma: extensions = [vector, pg_trgm]
5. Created deploy-interpreter-tables.sql idempotent deploy script

**Why**: The agents DB in production had the tables (medical_guides, exam_normalization_mappings) but pg_trgm was never installed and medical_guides was empty (0 rows). The reference-range-db.service.ts uses pg_trgm's similarity() function as fallback when vector search fails.

**Where**:
- Supabase project: lthmzgkdtpqotdrpk

observations.title: natales.travel architecture and design decisions
observations.content:
## natales.travel — Destination Portal for Puerto Natales / Torres del Paine

**What**: Independent travel destination portal targeting English-speaking tourists. Aggregates local experiences, accommodation, dining, and travel guides.

**Key decisions**:
- Architecture designed for full provider platform (Phase C), MVP launches as curated editorial portal (Phase B)
- Stack: Next.js 15 (App Router) + PostgreSQL (no Supabase) + Prisma + next-intl + Tailwind + shadcn/ui
- Hosting: Railway for production
- Images: Cloudinary
- Brand: Fully independent from Estepa Patagónica. Estepa appears as "recommended" provider, not owner.
- Visual: Premium & Cinematic dark theme (NatGeo meets luxury travel). Light mode for long-form editorial content.
- Homepage: Full immersive hero with mountain silhouet

observations.title: Implementado método Karpathy completo en vault Obsidian
observations.content:
**What**: Pipeline Karpathy end-to-end: _raw/ (fuentes inmutables) → COMPILATION_PROMPT.md (instrucciones) → LLM compiler (Minero/Cotocha/Claude) → Wiki/ (artículos compilados) → _INDEX.md + wiki-log.md (trazabilidad). Zonas formales: HUMANA (diario, inbox, proyecto), RAW (solo agregar), AI (Wiki/ — zona nativa de agentes).
**Why**: Cotocha identificó 7 gaps vs el método Karpathy (sin _raw/, sin ingest pipeline, sin separación human/AI, etc). Se resolvieron todos.
**Where**: _PROTOCOL_AGENTS.md (sección Zonas Karpathy), */COMPILATION_PROMPT.md (5 proyectos), */_raw/ (5 proyectos), */Wiki/_INDEX.md, 10_Investigación/Wiki/session_summary/wiki-log.md, 10_Investigación/Wiki/reflection/health-check-template.md
**Learned**: Los COMPILATION_PROMPT.md son el componente clave — sin ellos el LLM no

4. `ae70dfea` fix(interpreter): correct glucose normalMax 100→99 per MINSAL GPC

**Why**: JUDGE-INTERP-01 scored 1/5 (hemogram shown as "Examen de Orina"), production Supabase agents DB missing pg_trgm and MINSAL guide data, OCR eval tests failing in regular suite, glucose range wrong.

**Where**:
- interpret-results.handler.ts — accepts both detectedExamType and examType keys
- reference-

observations.title: Unified exam result interpretation — eliminated duplicate Path B
observations.content:
**What**: Eliminated duplicate exam result interpretation path (Path B) in whatsapp-webhook.controller.ts. When document classifier detects `exam_result`, it now calls `handleResultsPhotoOcr()` (same as stage-gated Path A) instead of the old `ExamResultWhatsappService.interpretAndSend()`.

**Why**: Two paths existed for interpreting exam results with different quality levels:
- Path A (new, stage-gated via `awaiting_results_photo`): gpt-4o + MINSAL guides + corrected parser + Chilean reference ranges
- Path B (old, classifier-gated): gpt-4o-mini, generic prompt, no MINSAL guides, no parser fixes, `max_tokens` not gpt-5 compatible

A user who sent a results photo without first requesting "interpretar resultados" got the inferior Path B. Now both paths produce the same high-quality output.

observations.title: OCR routing architecture: stage → classifier → quotation/interpretation
observations.content:
**What**: Complete routing logic for when a photo arrives via WhatsApp. Single decision point in `whatsapp-webhook.controller.ts:1189-1227`.

Flow:
1. Stage check: `stage == 'awaiting_results_photo'` → handleResultsPhotoOcr() → ExamResultInterpreterAgent (RETURN, skip classifier)
2. Classifier: `documentClassifier.classifyDocumentFromStorage()`
   - `exam_result` → handleResultsPhotoOcr() → ExamResultInterpreterAgent (unified with step 1 via PR #331)
   - `medical_order` → Quotation OCR → FONASA enrichment → payment link
   - `!isMedical` → "No es documento médico válido"
   - Classifier FAILS OPEN as `medical_order` → unknown docs go to quotation, never interpretation

Key rules:
- Stage has priority over classifier (line 1190 returns before classifier runs)
- NO `awaiting_order_photo` st

observations.title: natales.travel MVP implementation completed
observations.content:
## natales.travel MVP — Implementation Complete

**What**: Built the full MVP for natales.travel destination portal.

**Stack (actual, differs slightly from plan)**:
- Next.js 16 (not 15 — latest at time of install)
- Tailwind CSS v4 (CSS-based config via @theme inline, NO tailwind.config.ts)
- Prisma 6.19 with PostgreSQL (local, not Docker — port conflict resolved by using local postgres)
- next-intl for i18n (EN default, ES with /es/ prefix)
- next-mdx-remote for guide content rendering
- Vitest for testing (25 tests passing)

**Key deviations from plan**:
- Tailwind v4 uses CSS config, not JS config — custom colors/fonts in globals.css @theme block
- Prisma 6 auto-generated prisma.config.ts was removed (caused type errors, not needed)
- vitest.config.ts needed `react() as any` cast due
